sources.name: Sonic Physics Guide (SPG) — Jumping
sources.notes: Jump force values: Sonic/Tails 6.5 px/f (1664 sub-px), Knuckles 6.0 px/f. Gravity 0.21875 px/f². Variable height: release caps Y at -4. SPG treats S1-S3K as unified physics.

sources.name: Sonic Physics Guide (SPG) — Running
sources.notes: Core running constants confirmed identical to Sonic 1 and 2.

sources.name: Sonic Retro SPG — Characters
sources.notes: Character-specific mechanics: Knuckles glide, Tails flight, Sonic insta-shield.

notes: Sonic 3 & Knuckles is the combined locked-on version of Sonic 3 (1994) and Sonic & Knuckles (1994).
Core physics engine is IDENTICAL to Sonic 1 and 2 (same acc/dec/friction/top_speed/gravity/jump constants).
Key additions over Sonic 2: Knuckles as fully playable character with glide and wall climb, improved Tails flight, Insta-Shield for Sonic, Super/Hyper forms.
Knuckles has a lower jump force (6.0 px/f vs 6.5) compensated by glide.
All 256-subpixel hex values are inherited from Sonic 1 documentation (SPG unified).
_meta.release_note: Sonic 3 (Feb 1994) and Sonic & Knuckles (Oct 1994) are separate cartridges. The 'Lock-On Technology' connects them into S3&K — a single unified game with all zones and all characters. This entry documents the combined S3&K form.

ground_movement._source_note: All core constants identical to Sonic 1 and 2.

spin_dash.notes: Spin Dash mechanics unchanged from Sonic 2.

knuckles.notes: Knuckles jumps slightly lower than Sonic. Compensated by glide and wall climb.

insta_shield.description: Press jump while airborne to create a brief invincible shield flash that expands Sonic's hitbox.
notes: Insta-Shield is NEW in Sonic 3 — not in Sonic 1 or 2.
Destroys certain enemies and deflects projectiles on activation.
Resets after landing — one use per jump.
Changes approach to aerial combat: players can press jump mid-air offensively.

super_sonic.notes: Same mechanics as Sonic 2 Super Sonic.

hyper_sonic.notes: Hyper Sonic: even faster than Super Sonic, leaves afterimage, Insta-Shield becomes a full-screen flash that destroys all on-screen enemies.

flight.description: Tails can fly by pressing jump while airborne. Twin tails spin to generate lift.
flight.notes: In S3&K, Tails has extended flight duration compared to Sonic 2. Can fly indefinitely until he gets tired, then slowly descends.

super_tails.notes: Super Tails is invincible. Uniquely, Super Tails has Flicky bird helpers that automatically defeat nearby enemies.

hyper_tails.notes: Hyper Tails: same as Super Tails but with more Flicky companions.

glide.description: Press jump while airborne to initiate a glide. Knuckles descends slowly while maintaining horizontal speed.
glide.horizontal_speed_during_glide: maintains speed from before glide activation
notes: Glide allows crossing very large horizontal gaps — Knuckles can cover more horizontal distance than any other character on a single jump.
Can be steered left/right during glide.
Landing from glide at high speed causes Knuckles to stumble briefly.
Glide replaces the Insta-Shield (Knuckles cannot use Insta-Shield).

wall_climb.description: Knuckles can grab vertical walls and climb them by pressing up. Also slides down walls slowly without input.
notes: Wall climb gives Knuckles access to areas completely inaccessible to Sonic or Tails.
The combination of low jump + glide + wall climb gives Knuckles a distinct movement vocabulary: horizontal first, vertical second.
Knuckles' route through stages is intentionally different from Sonic's — same geometry, different traversal.

punch.description: Knuckles can punch forward to attack and break certain blocks.
punch.notes: Punch replaces Sonic's homing attack (not yet in S3K — introduced later in SA1).

super_knuckles.notes: Pink Super Knuckles — same speed boost and invincibility pattern.

hyper_knuckles.notes: Hyper Knuckles: glide slam creates a screen flash and earthquake effect.

design_notes_for_giants_drink: The three-character system in S3&K (Sonic, Tails, Knuckles) is the clearest example of 'same level geometry, radically different traversal.' Knuckles' route through every stage is specifically designed for his glide+climb combination. This is a masterclass in level reuse through movement vocabulary differences.

Knuckles' lower jump (6.0 vs 6.5) + glide + wall climb creates a horizontal-biased movement style. He trades vertical reach for horizontal range. Level design for Knuckles should have wide gaps, climbable walls, and areas accessible only from the sides.
Sonic's Insta-Shield adds an aggressive aerial dimension. Sonic players in S3K develop a jumping offense — pressing jump at enemy hitboxes is now a viable attack, not just accidental.

The Super/Hyper forms introduce a power scaling that turns the endgame into a different game. Collecting the emeralds is a meta-challenge that rewards thorough players with a mode where the normal challenge becomes trivial.

S3&K's level design is the most complex in the classic Genesis series — zones have three branching routes (upper/mid/lower) that separate based on the character's movement capabilities. This zone architecture is the gold standard for 'depth through movement.'
